avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Any new snow will be insignificant with the majority of the snow cover remaining soft but generally well bonded. However, in the afternoon wet snow instabilities may develop on the steepest terrain mostly on North-West through North-East to East aspects above 900 metres. Older snow will remain firm and well bonded. Cornice and icefall collapse will remain a hazard.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will drop to around 800 metres before rising above the summits. A mix of precipitation, light snow around dawn before rain at all levels during the day. Strong  winds, mostly South-South-West but backing South at end of day.
Observed Weather Influences: Cloudy on the hills with light rain during the morning but clearing in the afternoon with temperatures around +3 degrees at 900 metres. Strong winds throughout, South to South-South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of soft, wet but generally well bonded snow are found mostly in wind sheltered locations on North-West through North-East to East aspects above 900 metres. Old snow remains very firm and often icy. Stability of remaining cornices and ice-fall is suspect. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing patchy snow cover will remain well bonded and stable. Any new wet snow deposits on the highest summits will quickly thaw as the freezing level rises. Greatest areas will remain on North-West through North to East aspects above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Isolated snow showers overnight will become turn to rain at all levels by dawn and become more frequent during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild day with fresh South-South-Westerly winds. After a dry morning rain developed at all levels from mid afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: The patchy snow cover remains well bonded and stable. Greatest snow cover is on North-West through North to East aspects above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Snowpack stability will be generally good in all locations. In deep accumulations some localised instabilities will persist on very steep North and North-East aspects above 1000 meters but these will not be extensive. At higher elevations, the snowpack will firm up overnight before softening during the day. Snow loss will continue at lower elevations. Any remaining cornices will remain a hazard. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A dusting of fresh overnight snow above 1000 metres will clear to leave a dry day. Strong South-South-Westerly winds  will increase through the day backing South. The freezing level will be around 1000 metres overnight rising above summit height in the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Persistent morning drizzle above the cloud level cleared to leave a mainly dry afternoon with intermittent sunny breaks. Very strong winds from the South-South-West with the freezing level above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack is rapidly consolidating at all levels due to the mild conditions. In deep accumulations some localised instabilities still persist on North and North-East aspects above 1000 meters but these are not extensive. Most recent deposits are now soft in nature with the historical old snow pack remaining firm with only surface softening. Snow stability is generally good. Any remaining cornices remain a threat from collapse. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will be firm and stable on all aspects and elevations, the snow surface only softening late in the day. Any trace of snow at summit level is expected to be inconsequential. The largest continuous areas of snow will be on North-West through North to East aspects above 870 metres with ribbons and isolated patches below. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Precipitation is expected in the morning following a mostly dry night. This will fall as rain at all elevations with the exception of some sleet and wet snow reaching the highest summits. Winds will be mostly from the South, strong increasing to gale force by the end of the period. The freezing level will be around 900 metres overnight, rising to 1400 metres by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Dry with bright spells for most of the day with the exception of some light rain showers in the morning. Winds were Southerly fresh to strong. The freezing level was around 1300 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is firm and stable at all elevations above 850 metres, only softening at lower elevations. The largest continuous areas of snow are found on North-West through North to East aspects above 870 metres. Ribbons and isolated patches are found below this altitude. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack after firming up in the cooler overnight temperatures will continue to slowly thaw and diminish and will be well bonded and stable throughout.  Greatest amounts are mostly above 800m on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Some extensive snowfield remain in a few places on North facing coire back walls and in gullies. Patches below 800m and on other aspects.  Any new accumulations of summit snow are not expected to be significant.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Winds strong to gale South-Westerly backing Southerly in the afternoon when it is at its strongest. Freezing level around 900 metres overnight then rising to above the summits from daybreak. Precipitation falling as sleet and snow at summit levels which will diminish as temperatures rise by late afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild day with summit temperatures around 3 degrees Celsius midday. 
Showery in the morning falling as rain at all levels, Winds moderate strength South-Westerly.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to slowly thaw and deplete and is well bonded and stable throughout. Greatest amounts are mostly above 800m on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Some extensive snowfield remain in a places on North facing coire back walls and in gullies. Patchy below 800m and on other aspects. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will continue to thaw and consolidate. Stability will be good. Any fresh deposits are not expected to be significant. However, there are some cornices remaining above about 1100 metres on mainly North to East aspects.  These will soften and become less stable during the day. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will drop to around 900 metres overnight before rising again to 1400 metres during the day. There will be some light showers, initially falling as snow but soon turning to rain at all levels. Winds will be from the South.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a cloudy but generally dry day. The freezing rose from about 1000 metres overnight to about 1300 metres during the day. Winds were from the South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: The spring snowpack continues to slowly thaw and consolidate. The greatest deposits remain on North-West through North to East aspects above 800 metres. Stability is generally good throughout.  The avalanche hazard is Low.
